description:
Phase 2 of type_module_pass (lines 639-766) calls opaque_consumer_arities(m, &specs) at the top of every loop iteration. opaque_consumer_arities scans all registered specs and all their blocks — O(specs × blocks) — each time through the loop.

The result is stable once phase 1 has converged: new specs registered in phase 2 are MakeClosure-side any-key bodies whose callers were already walked in phase 1. They don't introduce new opaque consumers; the set of unresolved CallClosure sites doesn't change between phase-2 iterations.

Remove the per-iteration call. Compute opaque_consumer_arities once, after phase 1 converges, and pass the result into the phase-2 loop body.

Requires fz-afs.5 (S1) so opaque_consumer_arities accepts the shared idx_of.
title: remove per-iteration opaque_consumer_arities recomputation in phase 2
